key_insight: The Large Cancer Assistant (LCA) is a model-agnostic orchestration framework that separates multimodal data handling from AI inference to make oncology decision support more modular and scalable.
clinical_relevance: If validated in real-world settings, this approach could help hospitals integrate changing AI models and data sources without redesigning the entire clinical workflow.
method: Formalizes orchestration as a 7-tuple architecture with algorithmic impermeability.
Uses an Entry Theory and Geometric Deep Learning to standardize multimodal patient data.
Routes data through a Cancer Switching Module and outputs a Standardized Intermediate Payload (SIP).
evidence_signal: A proof-of-concept tested four technical scenarios and reported negligible orchestration overhead, invariant routing during model swaps, 100% recall for targeted supplementary data requests under injected anomalies, and multi-protocol execution.
watch_item: The results are based on a proof-of-concept, so clinical utility, robustness across hospitals, and EMR interoperability still need real-world validation.
paper_title: The Large Cancer Assistant (LCA): A Model-Agnostic Orchestration Framework for Scalable Clinical Decision Support in Oncology
